subject: Sociology
title: The Hidden Curriculum
description: A 60-second lesson on The Hidden Curriculum.

hook.text: The math lesson teaches algebra. But the bell, the rows, and the silence teach something else entirely.

buildup.text: The hidden curriculum refers to unwritten lessons: obedience, punctuality, competition, and respect for authority.

discovery.text: Students learn to wait their turn, follow rules, and accept hierarchy — skills factories and offices demand.

twist.text: Different schools teach different hidden lessons. Elite schools teach leadership; poor schools teach compliance.

climax.text: The hidden curriculum shapes attitudes and behaviors that determine class position as much as grades do.

punchline.text: The real lesson is never on the board.

quiz.question: What is the hidden curriculum?
options: Unwritten lessons about behavior and social norms
